name: AAPL Analyst Rating Asymmetry
description:
LOAD AAPL daily bars and analyst ratings data
DETECT price target raises (upgrades) and cuts (downgrades) via adjusted_pt_current > adjusted_pt_prior
CLASSIFY as Upgrade vs Downgrade
COMPUTE day-of and post-5/10/20d returns by direction
DASHBOARD shows event counts (summary table), asymmetry bar chart, window breakdown table, and event markers with PT change details

name: AAPL Gross Margin Expansion vs Contraction Drift
description:
LOAD AAPL daily bars and quarterly income_statement data
DETECT quarterly filings via is_valid(gross_profit)
CLASSIFY as Expanding (current gross margin > prior quarter) vs Contracting
COMPUTE day-of and post-5/10/20d returns by regime
DASHBOARD shows event counts (summary table), expansion vs contraction bar chart, window breakdown table, and event markers with margin details

name: AAPL Post-Earnings Drift with Analyst Confirmation
description:
LOAD AAPL daily bars, quarterly earnings, and analyst ratings
DETECT earnings events via eps_surprise_percent
CLASSIFY as Confirmed Beat (surprise > 0 + PT raise within 5 days), Confirmed Miss (surprise < 0 + PT cut), or Unconfirmed
COMPUTE day-of and post-5/10/20d returns by regime
DASHBOARD shows event counts (summary table), regime comparison bar chart, window breakdown table, and event markers

name: Mega-Cap Tech Valuation: Historical Z-Scores
description:
QUESTION Is mega-cap tech valuation stretched vs its own history?
LOAD 6 mega-cap tech stocks (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA) with income_statement and balance_sheet data
COMPUTE P/E and EV/EBITDA rolling 1-year z-scores (252-day window) for each stock
DASHBOARD shows cross-sectional z-score bar chart ranked by stretch, summary table with current P/E, mean, and z-score per stock

name: Mega-Cap Tech Valuation Snapshot
description:
QUESTION Who's cheapest among mega-cap tech?
LOAD 6 mega-cap tech stocks with income_statement and balance_sheet data
COMPUTE P/E, P/S, and EV/EBITDA at latest date for each stock
RANK stocks by each multiple
DASHBOARD shows cross-sectional bars for P/E, P/S, and EV/EBITDA ranked by value, plus summary table

name: What P/E Has AMZN Typically Traded At?
description:
QUESTION What is AMZN's typical P/E range, and where does it sit today?
CHART histogram showing P/E distribution (clamped 0-200x) with show_analytics=True
TABLE summary_table with Current, Median, Mean, Min, Max P/E using built-in aggregations
DATASET market_data_source (AMZN-Stocks, 1D) + income_statement (TTM)
KEY INSIGHT AMZN's median P/E is ~126x with wide historical range (28-183x), revealing how far current valuation sits from typical levels

name: CT Market Stats: Overnight, IB & Close -- ES Futures
description:
LOAD ES-Futures 1Min continuous contract via Polygon
COMPUTE session windows for RTH (09:30-16:00), ETH overnight (18:00-09:30), and IB (09:30-10:30)
CALCULATE overnight high/low/mid and volume profile (VPOC, VAH, VAL) touch rates during RTH
CALCULATE IB break probabilities: single/both/neither, within prior IB, opens-in-prior-IB breakout rates
CALCULATE close location after single IB break (above IBH, above MID, below IBL, below MID)
CALCULATE close stats: bearish/bullish engulfing, closes within IB on neutral day, within prior IB/range
DASHBOARD shows four summary tables: Overnight Session Levels, Initial Balance, Close After Single IB Break, Close

name: CT Market Stats: Auction & Opening Location -- ES Futures
description:
LOAD ES-Futures 1Min continuous contract via Polygon with RTH and IB session windows
COMPUTE 20-session harmonic rotations using zigzag swing detection (swing_length=2)
CALCULATE neutral day rate (both IB extremes broken), consecutive neutral day probability, and neutral extreme day rate
CALCULATE opening location rates: opens above/below pClose, above pHOD, below pLOD, within pIB, within pValue
DASHBOARD shows Auction summary table, Opening Location summary table, and Up/Down rotation histograms

name: CT Market Stats: Open vs Previous Range -- ES Futures
description:
LOAD ES-Futures 1Min continuous contract with RTH volume profile (VPOC, VAH, VAL)
CLASSIFY each session open as above, within, or below previous day RTH range
CALCULATE touch probabilities for 11 prior-session levels (pClose, pHOD, pLOD, pIBH, pIBL, pMID, pOpen, pVAH, pVAL, pVPOC, half-gap)
CONDITION touch rates on opening classification (above/within/below previous range)
DASHBOARD shows three summary tables: Opens Above, Opens Within, and Opens Below Previous Range with per-level touch rates

name: CT Market Stats: Volume & Range Distributions -- ES Futures
description:
LOAD ES-Futures 1Min continuous contract with RTH, ETH, and IB session windows aggregating volume
CALCULATE session-level volume (RTH and ETH) and range (RTH, ETH, IB) per day
COMPUTE mean, median, and standard deviation for each range distribution
DASHBOARD shows sample/range statistics table, RTH and ETH volume histograms, RTH and ETH range histograms, and IB range histogram

name: CT IB & Close Analysis
description:
LOAD SPY-Stocks 5Min bars as ES futures proxy with RTH and 30-min IB session windows
CALCULATE IB break probabilities: at-least-one, both, neither, only-IBH, only-IBL, IB-within-prior-IB
CALCULATE conditional close location after single IBH or IBL break (closes above/below IBH/IBL/MID)
CALCULATE close stats: bearish/bullish engulfing, closes within IB on neutral day, within prior IB/range
DASHBOARD shows overview table, IB Break Probabilities table, Close After Single IB Break table, and Close Statistics table

name: CT Opening & Level Touch
description:
LOAD SPY-Stocks 5Min bars with RTH, 30-min IB, and pre-market (04:00-09:30) session windows plus volume profile
CALCULATE opening location rates: above/below pClose, above pHOD, below pLOD, within pIB, within pValue
CLASSIFY opens as above/within/below prior range and compute conditional touch rates for 10+ prior levels
CALCULATE overnight session level touch rates: ONH, ONL, ONMID, ONVAH, ONVAL, ONVPOC
DASHBOARD shows Opening Location table, three conditional touch-rate tables (Above/Within/Below), and Overnight Level touch table

name: CT Session Structure
description:
LOAD SPY-Stocks 5Min extended-hours bars with RTH, pre-market, and IB session windows
COMPUTE harmonic rotations via zigzag swing detection: up/down rotation sizes, counts per session, session efficiency
CALCULATE auction day type: neutral day rate, neutral-follows-neutral probability, neutral extreme day rate
CALCULATE RTH and pre-market volume/range distributions plus IB range distributions
DASHBOARD shows summary table, rotation histograms (up/down/efficiency/count), auction table, volume histograms and stats, range histograms with boxplot comparison

name: Avoid Waiting for "the Dip"
description:
QUESTION How long do investors typically have to wait between 5% pullbacks in the S&P 500, and is waiting for a dip a reliable strategy?
CHART xy_lines (area) showing trading days since last 5% pullback over time
DATASET market_data_source (SPY-Stocks)
KEY INSIGHT The time between 5% pullbacks is highly variable (days to 400+ trading days), making market timing impractical

name: Historically, Bitcoin Returns Have Come With Volatility
description:
QUESTION How does Bitcoin's return and volatility profile compare to traditional asset classes like stocks, bonds, and gold?
CHART xy_bars showing annualized returns per asset class with diamond overlay for average drawdowns
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY, GLD, AGG), common_crypto_pairs (BTCUSD)
KEY INSIGHT Bitcoin leads with 20.9% annualized return but suffers 50%+ average drawdowns, far exceeding traditional assets

name: Volatility in Bonds is Historically Normal In All Years
description:
QUESTION How volatile are bonds within a given year, and do intra-year drawdowns in bonds typically recover by year-end?
CHART xy_bars showing annual return (blue) and largest intra-year drawdown (red) per year labeled by year
DATASET common_reference_stocks (AGG)
KEY INSIGHT Bonds averaged 5.21% annual return with -3.53% average intra-year drawdown from 1990-2025, showing temporary declines are normal

name: Is the Yield Curve Inverted or Uninverted?
description:
QUESTION Is the U.S. Treasury yield curve currently inverted, and what does the 10-year minus 2-year spread tell us about recession risk?
CHART xy_lines (area) showing yield spread split into positive (uninverted, blue) and negative (inverted, red) regions
DATASET common_economic_indicators (Spread10Y2Y)
KEY INSIGHT The yield curve was inverted from mid-2022 through late 2024, one of the longest inversions in modern history, before normalizing

name: Federal Funds Rate and FOMC Projection
description:
QUESTION Where is the federal funds rate currently, and what does the FOMC project for future rate levels?
CHART xy_lines showing actual effective fed funds rate (solid) and FOMC median projection (dashed) over time
DATASET common_economic_indicators (FedFunds), economic_indicators (FEDTARMD)
KEY INSIGHT The dramatic hiking cycle from near-zero to above 5% in 2022-2023 is followed by projected gradual rate reductions through 2028

name: Major Country Equity Performance in 2025
description:
QUESTION How did major country equity markets perform in 2025, and which countries delivered the strongest and weakest returns?
CHART xy_bars showing YTD total return for 10 countries via ETF proxies (anchored to specific year)
DATASET market_data_source (SPY), reference_stocks (EWZ, EWA, EWJ, EWU, EWC, EWQ, EWG, FXI, INDA)
KEY INSIGHT International markets broadly outperformed the U.S. in 2025, with Brazil leading at 48.5% and India lagging at 2.7%

name: Major Country Equity Performance This Year
description:
QUESTION How are major country equity markets performing year-to-date, and which countries are leading or lagging?
CHART xy_bars showing YTD total return for 10 countries via ETF proxies (rolling current year)
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY), reference_stocks (EWZ, EWA, EWJ, EWU, EWC, EWQ, EWG, FXI, INDA)
KEY INSIGHT International diversification continues to show value as non-U.S. markets frequently outperform U.S. equities in individual years

name: Cycles of Gold vs S&P 500 Performance
description:
QUESTION How has the relative performance of gold versus the S&P 500 evolved over rolling 3-year periods, and where does it stand now?
CHART xy_lines (area) showing rolling 3-year return spread (Gold minus S&P 500) over time
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY, GLD)
KEY INSIGHT Relative performance between gold and equities is cyclical, with gold currently outperforming the S&P 500 by over 100% on a 3-year rolling basis

name: Volatility in a 60/40 is Historically Normal in All Years
description:
QUESTION How common are intra-year drawdowns in a balanced 60/40 portfolio, and do they prevent positive annual returns?
CHART xy_bars showing annual return (color_by_value bars) with diamond overlay for largest intra-year drawdown, labeled by year
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY, AGG)
KEY INSIGHT The 60/40 portfolio averaged 9.3% annual return with -9.9% average intra-year drawdown from 1990-2024, showing temporary pullbacks are normal

name: Job Loss in the United States
description:
QUESTION What do initial unemployment claims tell us about the current state of the U.S. labor market, and how do they compare historically?
CHART xy_lines showing weekly initial unemployment claims (ICSA) in thousands of persons over time
DATASET economic_indicators(series_id='ICSA')
KEY INSIGHT Current claims at ~212k remain at historically low levels, suggesting a stable labor market despite the massive COVID-era spike that dwarfed all prior recessions

name: Major U.S. Index Performance in 2025
description:
QUESTION What were the total returns for the S&P 500, Dow, and NASDAQ Composite for the full year 2025?
CHART xy_bars showing full-year 2025 total return for SPY, DIA, QQQ
DATASET market_data_source (SPY), common_reference_stocks (DIA, QQQ)
KEY INSIGHT NASDAQ led at 21.1%, followed by S&P 500 at 17.9% and Dow at 14.9%, with all three posting strong double-digit gains

name: Major U.S. Index Performance This Year
description:
QUESTION What are the year-to-date returns for the major U.S. stock indices, and how do they compare?
CHART xy_bars showing YTD total return for SPY, DIA, QQQ
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY, DIA, QQQ)
KEY INSIGHT Dow leads YTD at 2.1% while NASDAQ lags at -2.4%, signaling potential rotation away from growth toward value

name: Major U.S. Index Performance Path This Year
description:
QUESTION How are the major U.S. stock indices performing so far this year, and which index is leading or lagging?
CHART xy_lines showing YTD cumulative return path for SPY, DIA, QQQ
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY, DIA, QQQ)
KEY INSIGHT All three indices show choppy range-bound trading with no clear sustained uptrend, and NASDAQ underperforms reflecting tech weakness

name: Major U.S. Index Performance Path In 2025
description:
QUESTION How did the major U.S. stock indices perform over the course of 2025, and what was the trajectory of their returns?
CHART xy_lines showing daily cumulative return path for SPY, DIA, QQQ during 2025
DATASET market_data_source (SPY), common_reference_stocks (DIA, QQQ)
KEY INSIGHT All three indices experienced notable mid-year drawdowns of 10-20% before recovering strongly, with NASDAQ pulling ahead in the final months

name: A Look at Monthly Job Growth
description:
QUESTION How has monthly U.S. job growth trended recently, and what does the 3-month moving average reveal about the labor market's trajectory?
CHART xy_bars with line overlay showing monthly nonfarm payroll changes (bars) and 3-month moving average (dashed line)
DATASET economic_indicators(series_id='PAYEMS')
KEY INSIGHT The 3-month moving average shows a downward trend from mid-2024 levels, suggesting the labor market is gradually cooling

name: Trends in Consumption
description:
QUESTION What do the headline and core PCE inflation measures indicate about current consumer-level inflationary pressures?
CHART xy_lines showing year-over-year change in Headline PCE and Core PCE
DATASET common_economic_indicators (PCE, CorePCE)
KEY INSIGHT Both Headline PCE (2.9%) and Core PCE (3.0%) remain above the Fed's 2% target despite declining substantially from 2021-2022 peaks

name: # of 10% Pullbacks by Different Stock / Bond Splits
description:
QUESTION How does the stock/bond allocation mix in a portfolio affect the frequency of 10% or greater pullbacks?
CHART xy_bars showing count of 10% pullbacks for 11 stock/bond allocations from 100/0 to 0/100
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY, AGG)
KEY INSIGHT A 100% stock portfolio experienced 14 pullbacks of 10%+ since 1990 versus only 1 for a 10/90 or 0/100 bond-heavy portfolio, demonstrating the risk-dampening effect of bonds

name: # of 5% Pullbacks by Different Stock / Bond Splits
description:
QUESTION How does the stock-to-bond allocation in a portfolio affect the frequency of 5% pullbacks?
CHART xy_bars showing count of 5% pullbacks for 11 stock/bond allocations from 100/0 to 0/100
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY, AGG)
KEY INSIGHT A roughly linear decline in pullback frequency as bond allocation increases, from 42 pullbacks at 100/0 to just 4 at 10/90, illustrating the risk-reward tradeoff

name: Win Ratio by Different Stock / Bond Splits
description:
QUESTION How does the stock-to-bond allocation ratio impact the win ratio (percentage of positive yearly returns)?
CHART xy_bars showing win ratio for 11 stock/bond allocation splits from 100/0 to 0/100
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY, AGG)
KEY INSIGHT Moderate bond allocations (20-80% bonds) historically increase the frequency of positive return years, with the 20/80 split achieving the highest win ratio at ~91.8%

name: Trends in Producer Prices
description:
QUESTION What are the recent trends in producer-level inflation, and how do headline and core PPI compare?
CHART xy_lines showing year-over-year change in Headline PPI vs Core PPI as area chart
DATASET common_economic_indicators (PPI, CorePPI)
KEY INSIGHT Producer prices spiked above 12% YoY in 2021-2022 before normalizing, and core PPI has recently exceeded headline PPI indicating persistent underlying price pressures

name: Quits vs Hires in the U.S. Economy
description:
QUESTION How do voluntary quits compare to total hires in the U.S. labor market, and what does this ratio signal about worker confidence and labor demand?
CHART xy_lines showing quit rate and hire rate over time
DATASET common_economic_indicators (QuitRate, HireRate)
KEY INSIGHT Hires consistently outpace quits, and the spread between them normalized to pre-pandemic levels after the dramatic COVID-19 disruption

name: Historical Growth of the U.S. Economy
description:
QUESTION How has the overall size of the U.S. economy grown over the past seven decades in real (inflation-adjusted) terms?
CHART xy_lines area chart showing U.S. Real GDP in trillions of dollars
DATASET common_economic_indicators (RealGDP)
KEY INSIGHT U.S. Real GDP has grown to over $24 trillion following an exponential growth curve, with a brief but visible contraction during the 2020 pandemic

name: How is the U.S. Economy Performing?
description:
QUESTION What has the recent trajectory of U.S. GDP growth looked like, and what does the Atlanta Fed's GDPNow model forecast for upcoming quarters?
CHART xy_bars showing quarterly Real GDP annualized growth rates with QuarterYear labels
DATASET economic_indicators (A191RL1Q225SBEA - BEA GDP growth rate)
KEY INSIGHT Growth has been uneven, ranging from 4.7% (Q2 2023) to a contraction of -0.6% in Q1 2025, reflecting shifting conditions from interest rate impacts and consumer spending patterns

name: Retail Sales
description:
QUESTION How have monthly retail sales been trending, and what does the recent data suggest about consumer spending strength?
CHART xy_bars showing month-over-month percentage change in retail sales by month with color by value
DATASET common_economic_indicators (RetailSales)
KEY INSIGHT Retail sales showed notable variability throughout 2025, with consistent growth suggesting economic strength while prolonged weakness may signal consumer hesitation

name: How Has The S&P 500 Performed During Government Shutdowns?
description:
QUESTION Do government shutdowns historically lead to negative stock market returns?
CHART xy_bars showing S&P 500 return during each government shutdown period since 2013
DATASET market_data_source (SPY-Stocks)
KEY INSIGHT In 8 of 11 past shutdowns since 1980, the S&P 500 generated positive returns, showing markets have often looked past the political noise

name: Historically, All-Time Highs Tend to Cluster
description:
QUESTION How frequently does the S&P 500 reach new all-time highs, and do they tend to cluster together?
CHART xy_bars showing number of new all-time highs per year with average reference line at 24
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY)
KEY INSIGHT All-time highs cluster together during strong market periods, with years like 1995, 2014, 2017, and 2021 seeing especially high concentrations, reinforcing that new highs are a normal feature of long-term growth

name: Average Calendar-Year Path of the S&P 500
description:
QUESTION How does the S&P 500's performance in 2026 compare to the average calendar-year path since 1950?
CHART xy_lines showing 2026 YTD path vs historical average calendar-year return path by day of year
DATASET market_data_source (SPY-Stocks)
KEY INSIGHT The historical average path shows a general upward trend through the year ending around 9-10%, with the bulk of gains typically accruing in the second half

name: Average Post-Election Year Path of the S&P 500
description:
QUESTION How does the S&P 500 typically perform during post-election years, and how is 2026 tracking against that historical pattern?
CHART xy_lines showing 2026 YTD path vs historical average post-election year path by day of year
DATASET market_data_source (SPY-Stocks)
KEY INSIGHT Post-election years follow a distinct seasonal pattern with heightened uncertainty early and improving momentum later, though individual years often deviate meaningfully from the average

name: Average Return by Month in the S&P 500
description:
QUESTION Which months have historically been the strongest and weakest for the S&P 500, and what seasonal patterns exist?
CHART xy_bars showing average monthly return for each calendar month
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY)
KEY INSIGHT November (1.85%), April (1.43%), and December (1.39%) are historically the strongest months while September (-0.62%) is the only month with a negative average return

name: Gains Increase with Holding Period
description:
QUESTION How do average S&P 500 returns change as the investment holding period increases?
CHART xy_bars showing average returns across 9 holding periods from 1 day to 20 years
DATASET market_data_source (SPY-Stocks)
KEY INSIGHT Average returns increase dramatically with longer holding periods from 0.04% (1 day) to 322.66% (20 years), demonstrating the critical importance of time in the market

name: Most Years See Big Gains or Big Losses
description:
QUESTION How do average gains in up years compare to average losses in down years for the S&P 500?
CHART xy_bars showing average up-year return (+17.7%) vs average down-year return (-11.8%)
DATASET market_data_source (SPY-Stocks)
KEY INSIGHT Big swings in either direction are normal market behavior, with average up years returning +17.7% and average down years declining -11.8%

name: Historically, The Best and Worst Days Have Clustered
description:
QUESTION Do the best and worst days in the stock market occur randomly, or do they tend to cluster together?
CHART xy_lines with event_marker overlays showing S&P 500 price with best/worst daily returns highlighted as dots
DATASET market_data_source (SPY-Stocks)
KEY INSIGHT The 25 best and 25 worst daily performances cluster around periods of heightened volatility, and many best days occur shortly after the worst ones

name: Bond vs Stock Historical Drawdowns
description:
QUESTION How do the worst drawdowns in bonds compare to the worst drawdowns in stocks over the same time period?
CHART xy_lines showing drawdown time series for bonds (AGG) vs stocks (SPY)
DATASET market_data_source (SPY-Stocks), common_reference_stocks (AGG)
KEY INSIGHT Stock drawdowns have exceeded -50% while bond drawdowns have generally stayed above -20%, illustrating bonds' relative stability as a portfolio diversifier

name: 5% Intra-Year Pullbacks Have Been Historically Common
description:
QUESTION How frequently do 5% pullbacks occur within a given year in the S&P 500?
CHART xy_bars showing annual count of 5%+ intra-year pullback episodes
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY)
KEY INSIGHT 5% pullbacks are a normal part of market behavior with most years experiencing at least one, ranging from 0 to 5 pullbacks per year since 1990

name: A Short-Term View Of Stocks
description:
QUESTION How has the S&P 500 performed over the short term, and what does its recent price action look like?
CHART xy_lines showing S&P 500 index level over the past two years
DATASET market_data_source (SPY-Stocks)
KEY INSIGHT The S&P 500 rose from approximately 4,800 in early 2024 to over 6,000 by early 2026 despite multiple significant pullbacks, underscoring that short-term volatility is normal

name: A Decade's Worth of Volatility In The 2020s
description:
QUESTION How does the frequency of large daily moves in the S&P 500 during the 2020s compare to prior decades?
CHART xy_bars showing total count of 1%+ up and down days by decade
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY)
KEY INSIGHT The 2020s have already logged 840 days with 1%+ moves, surpassing the entire decades of the 1950s (227) and 1960s (315) and exceeding the long-term average of 493

name: Ignoring Politics When Investing
description:
QUESTION Does the political party of the president significantly impact long-term S&P 500 performance?
CHART xy_lines showing S&P 500 price on log scale colored by presidential party (Democrats blue, Republicans red)
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY)
KEY INSIGHT The S&P 500 has grown substantially under both Democratic and Republican administrations, making politics more of a distraction than a signal for long-term investors

name: A Manual for Bear Markets
description:
QUESTION How severe have past bear markets been, and what is the average decline investors should expect?
CHART xy_bars showing max drawdown for each dynamically detected bear market with -35% average reference line
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY)
KEY INSIGHT Bear market declines have ranged from -22% to -57%, averaging approximately -35%, reinforcing the importance of staying disciplined during downturns

name: A Manual for Bull Markets
description:
QUESTION How do S&P 500 returns during historical bull markets compare to each other and to the current bull market?
CHART xy_bars showing max return from trough for each dynamically detected bull market with 192% average reference line
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY)
KEY INSIGHT Bull markets have averaged 192% returns, and the current bull since October 2022 at ~92% suggests potential room for further growth

name: Putting Numbers Behind The Santa Claus Rally
description:
QUESTION Is the Santa Claus Rally a real seasonal pattern, and how significant are the returns during this period?
CHART xy_bars showing average daily returns for each of the 7 trading days in the Santa Claus Rally window plus cumulative return
DATASET market_data_source (SPY-Stocks)
KEY INSIGHT The S&P 500 has posted positive average returns across all 7 days of the Santa Claus Rally window since 1950, with an average cumulative return of approximately +1.27%

name: All Time Highs in the S&P 500 are Common
description:
QUESTION How frequently does the S&P 500 reach new all-time highs, and should investors be concerned when the market hits a new peak?
CHART xy_lines showing S&P 500 price on log scale with circle markers on all-time high days
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY)
KEY INSIGHT New all-time highs are a regular and frequent occurrence across every decade, and reaching an ATH is not a reason to sell

name: The Importance of Dividends
description:
QUESTION How much of an impact do reinvested dividends have on long-term S&P 500 investment returns?
CHART xy_lines showing growth of $1 with dividends reinvested vs price-only since 1990
DATASET market_data_source (SPY-Stocks), dividends
KEY INSIGHT Reinvesting dividends roughly doubled the total return over this period, with $1 growing to ~$40 vs ~$19 price-only

name: Cycles of International vs U.S. Performance
description:
QUESTION Do international stocks and U.S. stocks take turns outperforming each other, and what is the current trend?
CHART xy_lines showing rolling 1-year return spread (EFA minus SPY) with dual-color area fill
DATASET market_data_source (SPY-Stocks), reference_stocks (EFA)
KEY INSIGHT The performance spread is highly cyclical, swinging from +25% international outperformance to -30% U.S. outperformance, supporting geographic diversification

name: S&P 500 with Recession Periods
description:
QUESTION How has the S&P 500 performed through recessions over the long term?
CHART xy_lines showing S&P 500 on log scale with gray recession period band shading
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY), common_economic_indicators (RecessionProb)
KEY INSIGHT Despite recurring recessions, the S&P 500 long-term trajectory has been decisively upward, with the market historically bouncing back and surpassing previous highs

name: Staying The Course Worked In 2025
description:
QUESTION What was the cost of selling at the April 2025 lows versus staying invested across different equity categories?
CHART xy_bars showing paired held-vs-sold returns across 5 equity categories (Emerging Markets, Developed Markets, U.S. Large Cap, Mid Cap, Small Cap)
DATASET market_data_source (SPY-Stocks), reference_stocks (EEM, EFA, MDY, IWM)
KEY INSIGHT Investors who stayed invested were rewarded across all equity categories in 2025, while panic sellers locked in -10% to -30% losses

name: A Tactical Approach Can Lead To Significant Gains
description:
QUESTION How much could investment returns improve if the worst market days were avoided?
CHART xy_lines showing growth of $1 in the S&P 500 under three scenarios: all days, missing worst 50, missing worst 100
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY)
KEY INSIGHT Missing the worst 100 days since 1950 would have turned $1 into ~$66,168 vs $413 staying invested, highlighting the theoretical power of tactical loss avoidance

name: Time Between S&P 500 All Time Highs
description:
QUESTION How long has it typically taken for the S&P 500 to recover and set a new all-time high after the previous one?
CHART xy_lines (area) showing trading days between each new S&P 500 all-time high
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY)
KEY INSIGHT Post-2000 and post-2007, investors waited over 1,500 trading days (~6 years) for new highs, reinforcing long-term patience

name: How Often The S&P 500 Has Stayed Near Record Highs
description:
QUESTION How often and for how long has the S&P 500 remained close to its all-time highs throughout history?
CHART xy_lines (area) showing consecutive trading days the S&P 500 stays within 3% of its all-time high
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY)
KEY INSIGHT The late 1990s saw the longest streak of 350+ consecutive days near ATH; current streaks are modest by comparison

name: A Long-Term View Of U.S. Treasury Yields
description:
QUESTION How have the 2-year and 10-year U.S. Treasury yields behaved over the past several decades, and where do they stand now?
CHART xy_lines showing 10-Year and 2-Year Treasury yields over the long term since 1988
DATASET common_economic_indicators (Treasury2Y, Treasury10Y)
KEY INSIGHT A secular decline in yields from late 1980s through 2020 reversed sharply in 2022-2023 as the Fed raised rates to combat inflation

name: A Short-Term View Of U.S. Treasury Yields
description:
QUESTION How have 2-year and 10-year U.S. Treasury yields moved over the past two years, and what is the current relationship between them?
CHART xy_lines showing 10-Year and 2-Year Treasury yields over the short term (past 2 years)
DATASET common_economic_indicators (Treasury2Y, Treasury10Y)
KEY INSIGHT The yield curve uninverted as the 2-year yield fell from above 5% to ~3.39% while the 10-year remained anchored around 3.5-4.8%

name: A Look at the U.S. Treasury Yield Curve
description:
QUESTION What does the current shape of the U.S. Treasury yield curve look like, and how has it changed from a year ago?
CHART labeled_lines showing yield curve (Current vs 1 Year Ago) across 11 maturities from 1M to 30Y, plus xy_lines showing 5 key yields over time
DATASET common_economic_indicators (Treasury1M through Treasury30Y, 11 maturities)
KEY INSIGHT The curve shifted from inverted to positively sloped, historically associated with improving economic outlooks and reduced recession risk

name: Asset Class Returns In 2026
description:
QUESTION How are different asset classes performing year-to-date in 2026, and which are leading or lagging?
CHART xy_bars showing YTD total returns for 12 asset classes (Gold, EM, Intl, S&P 500, Commodities, Mid Cap, Real Estate, Small Cap, Bonds, Growth, T-Bills, Bitcoin)
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY, GLD, IWM, AGG) + reference_stocks (EEM, EFA, DBC, MDY, VNQ, IVW, BIL, IBIT)
KEY INSIGHT Gold continues to lead YTD 2026 at 22.1% while Bitcoin lags at -25.0%, with international and commodity investments outperforming US equities

name: Asset Class Returns In 2025
description:
QUESTION How did different asset classes perform in 2025, and which were the best and worst performers?
CHART xy_bars showing full-year 2025 total returns for 11 asset classes (Gold, EM, Intl, S&P 500, Commodities, Mid Cap, Small Cap, Bonds, Growth, T-Bills, Bitcoin)
DATASET common_reference_stocks (SPY, GLD, IWM, AGG) + reference_stocks (EEM, EFA, DBC, MDY, IVW, BIL, IBIT)
KEY INSIGHT Gold led all asset classes in 2025 at 63.7% while Bitcoin was the worst at -6.6%, highlighting diversification value

name: Commodity Correlations (12M Weekly Returns)
description:
LOAD 20 commodity price series from FRED (metals, energy, grains, softs, meats, lumber) plus USD Index and SPY
COMPUTE weekly returns for all assets on a 1W timeframe
CALCULATE 52-week rolling Pearson correlations of each commodity to USD Index and to S&P 500
GENERATE 20-week rolling USD-Lumber correlation time series chart
DASHBOARD shows a labeled scatter plot with all 20 commodities positioned by USD correlation (X) vs S&P 500 correlation (Y)

name: Currency-Commodity Correlations Analysis
description:
LOAD FX pair (AUDUSD/USDCAD/USDCHF/USDJPY) daily data plus Gold (GLD), Oil (WTI), Iron Ore, and Silver reference series
CALCULATE 20-day rolling Pearson correlations between the FX pair and each commodity
VALIDATE reported correlations (e.g., AUDUSD/Gold ~0.80, CAD/Oil ~-0.87) against computed values
DASHBOARD shows four time-series charts (Gold, Oil, Iron Ore, Silver rolling correlations) with reference lines at reported values, plus summary table with mean/min/max correlation stats

name: Stock-Bond Correlation Regime Shifts
description:
LOAD SPY weekly returns and TLT (long-term Treasury) weekly returns via Polygon
CALCULATE 1-year, 2-year, and 3-year rolling Pearson stock-bond correlations
COMPUTE CPI and Industrial Production year-over-year changes, 2-year rolling volatility, and growth-inflation correlation
CLASSIFY macroeconomic regimes (growth up/down, inflation up/down) using expanding median thresholds
DASHBOARD shows SBC regime shift time series, inflation/growth uncertainty chart, growth-inflation correlation chart, and average stock/bond returns by growth and inflation regime bar charts

name: EUR/USD Monthly and Day-of-Week Seasonality
description:
QUESTION Does EUR/USD have exploitable monthly or day-of-week seasonality?
LOAD EUR/USD daily and monthly bars
COMPUTE monthly returns and intraweek returns
AGGREGATE average return by month, year-by-year heatmap, and day-of-week breakdown
DASHBOARD shows monthly seasonality bars, year×month heatmap, day-of-week bars, and win rate table

name: Gold Intraday Reaction to NFP Releases
description:
LOAD GC futures 1Min bars via futures_continuation and NFP data from common_economic_indicators
DETECT NFP release near 8:30 AM ET
CLASSIFY as Strong (change > 200K) vs Weak (< 100K)
COMPUTE 1-minute spike, 1-hour digestion, and full-day persistence returns by regime
DASHBOARD shows regime comparison bars, intraday window breakdown, cumulative return paths, and event markers

name: Gold Daily Reaction to Strong vs Weak NFP
description:
LOAD GC futures daily bars and NFP data from common_economic_indicators
DETECT NFP releases via valuewhen for prior reading
CLASSIFY as Strong (change > 200K) vs Weak (< 100K)
COMPUTE day-of and post-20d returns by regime with cumulative event returns
DASHBOARD shows event counts (summary table), regime comparison bars, window breakdown table, and event markers

name: Is MSFT's Earnings Growth Accelerating or Decelerating?
description:
QUESTION Is MSFT's earnings growth accelerating or decelerating?
CARD Last P/E Ratio — valuation context
CHART xy_bars showing quarterly YoY EPS growth rate with color_by_value and zero reference line
DATASET market_data_source (MSFT-Stocks, 1D) + income_statement (TTM diluted EPS)
KEY INSIGHT Rising bars = growth accelerating, shrinking bars = growth decelerating — tracks earnings momentum over time

name: Does MSFT Sell Off When Real Rates Rise?
description:
QUESTION Does MSFT underperform when real rates rise?
CHART 1 xy_lines showing MSFT price path colored by rate regime (rates falling = Blue, rates rising = Gray)
CHART 2 xy_bars showing cumulative MSFT return during each regime
DATASET market_data_source (MSFT-Stocks, 1D) + economic_indicators (DFII10 — 10Y TIPS real yield)
KEY INSIGHT Segments MSFT returns by whether 10Y TIPS real yield is above or below its 60-day SMA, capturing the full 2020-2024 rate cycle

name: What's Driving MSFT's Returns — Earnings, Rates, or Sentiment?
description:
QUESTION What's driving MSFT's returns — earnings growth, rate changes, or sentiment?
CHART 1 xy_bars showing cumulative contribution from each driver (Earnings Growth, Rate Impact, Sentiment)
CHART 2 xy_lines showing cumulative attribution over time
DATASET market_data_source (MSFT-Stocks, 1D) + income_statement (TTM) + economic_indicators (DFII10)
KEY INSIGHT Decomposes returns using Price = EPS × P/E identity with rolling 60-day beta to real yield changes

name: QQQ Failed IB Low Break Reversal Probability
description:
LOAD QQQ 5Min bars with session_window for IB (9:30-10:30) and RTH
DETECT failed downside breaks: price broke IB low AND prior day low
DEFINE win as close above IB midpoint
COMPUTE cumulative win rate line showing edge evolution over time
DASHBOARD shows win rate (summary table), cumulative win rate line, return distribution histogram, and event markers on reversals

name: QQQ IB Low Reversal Timing by Day-of-Week
description:
LOAD QQQ 5Min bars with session_window for IB and RTH
DETECT same failed IB low break trigger as reversal_probability study
SEGMENT by day-of-week
COMPUTE win/loss counts and win rate per weekday
DASHBOARD shows stacked win/loss bars per day, win rate bars by weekday, and day-of-week distribution

name: When Margins Are Elevated, How Often Do They Persist?
description:
QUESTION When margins are elevated (z>1 vs own 1Y history), how often do they decline the next quarter?
CARD P(Decline | Elevated) — cross-sectional mean across 30 mega-cap stocks
CHART cs_bars showing P(Decline) ranked by stock
TABLE cs_summary_table with Op Margin, 1Y Avg, Z-Score, QoQ change, P(Decline) for 30 stocks
DATASET market_data_source (30 mega-cap S&P 500 stocks, 1D) + income_statement (TTM)
KEY INSIGHT Tests Fama & French (1999) finding on margin mean reversion across sector-diverse mega-caps

name: SPY Reaction to Claims Spikes Conditioned on Yield Curve
description:
LOAD SPY daily bars, InitialClaims and Spread10Y2Y from common_economic_indicators
DETECT claims spikes (> 10% above 3-reading average)
CLASSIFY as Spike + Inverted Curve vs Spike + Normal Curve
COMPUTE day-of and post-20d returns by regime with cumulative compounding
DASHBOARD shows event counts (summary table), regime comparison bars, window breakdown table, and event markers

name: SPY Dividend Ex-Date Drop and Recovery
description:
LOAD SPY daily bars and dividend data via dividends(dividend_type=CD)
DETECT cash dividend ex-dates
CLASSIFY by yield tier: High (> 0.4%) vs Low
COMPUTE drop ratio (actual drop / expected dividend), ex-date day and post-5/10d returns
DASHBOARD shows yield tier counts (summary table), recovery bars by tier, window breakdown table, and event markers with drop ratio

name: SPY Reaction to Fed Rate Hikes vs Cuts
description:
LOAD SPY daily bars and FedFunds from common_economic_indicators
DETECT FOMC decisions via rate changes (valuewhen for prior rate)
CLASSIFY as Hike (>= 20bps), Cut (>= 20bps), or Hold
COMPUTE day-of and post-20d returns by regime with cumulative compounding
DASHBOARD shows event counts (summary table), regime comparison bars, window breakdown table, and event markers

name: SPY Intraday Reaction to ISM Manufacturing
description:
LOAD SPY 1Min bars and ISM Manufacturing from common_economic_indicators
DETECT ISM release near 10:00 AM ET
CLASSIFY as Improving (current > prior) vs Deteriorating
COMPUTE 1-minute spike, 1-hour digestion, and full-day persistence returns
DASHBOARD shows regime comparison bars, intraday window breakdown, cumulative return paths, and event markers

name: SPY Daily Reaction to Improving vs Deteriorating ISM
description:
LOAD SPY daily bars and ISM Manufacturing from common_economic_indicators
DETECT ISM releases via valuewhen for prior reading
CLASSIFY as Improving (current > prior) vs Deteriorating
COMPUTE day-of and post-20d returns by regime with cumulative event returns
DASHBOARD shows event counts (summary table), regime comparison bars, window breakdown table, and event markers

name: SPY Morning Dip Mean-Reversion Strategy
description:
LOAD SPY 5Min bars with session_window for RTH and 9:45-10:30 trade window
DETECT morning dip: price at 9:45 < 9:30 open
TRADE entry at 9:45, exit at 10:30 when triggered
COMPUTE cumulative equity curve, return distribution, and day-of-week breakdown
DASHBOARD shows win rate (summary table), equity curve line, return histogram, and day-of-week performance bars

name: SPY Opening Range Extension Analysis
description:
LOAD SPY 1Min bars with session_window for OR (9:30-9:45), RTH, and EOD
COMPUTE opening range high/low, then measure upside and downside extension beyond OR boundaries
ANALYZE distribution of extension distances via boxplot and histogram
DASHBOARD shows median extension (summary table), upside vs downside boxplots, extension distance histogram with analytics

name: SPY Quarterly OPEX Momentum Reversal Backtest
description:
LOAD SPY daily bars
DETECT quarterly OPEX Fridays (3rd Friday of Mar/Jun/Sep/Dec) via is_period_boundary
SIGNAL counter-momentum fade on OPEX Monday: long if 10-day momentum < 0, short if > 0
HOLD for 10 trading days after OPEX Friday
DASHBOARD shows cumulative equity curve and per-trade return distribution

name: SPY Quarterly OPEX Week Return Pattern
description:
QUESTION Is there a reliable SPY return pattern around quarterly OPEX?
LOAD SPY daily bars
DETECT quarterly OPEX Fridays via is_period_boundary (3rd Friday of Mar/Jun/Sep/Dec)
COMPUTE 5-day return on OPEX weeks, aggregate by quarter and year
DASHBOARD shows average return by quarter bars, year-by-year totals, quarter×year heatmap

name: SPY Reaction to Yield Curve Inversions and Un-Inversions
description:
LOAD SPY daily bars and 10Y-2Y spread from common_economic_indicators
DETECT zero-crossings: new inversion (spread crosses below 0) and un-inversion (crosses back above)
COMPUTE event day and post-60/126/252d returns by crossing type
DASHBOARD shows crossing counts (summary table), 1-year post-event bar chart, long-window breakdown table, and event markers

name: Stock-Bond Correlation: Diversification Regime Tracker
description:
LOAD SPY weekly bars and TLT via reference_stocks on 1W timeframe
COMPUTE weekly returns for both assets
CALCULATE 2-year (104-week) rolling Pearson correlation
SPLIT into negative (diversifying) vs positive (co-moving) regimes
DASHBOARD shows area chart with blue fill when negative, gray when positive, plus current correlation card

name: What Drives Stock-Bond Correlation Flips?
description:
LOAD SPY daily bars, TLT via reference_stocks, and CPI from common_economic_indicators
COMPUTE 1-year rolling stock-bond correlation (252-day) and CPI volatility (rolling 1-year stdev)
SPLIT by inflation uncertainty regime (high vs low CPI vol)
DASHBOARD shows scatter plot (CPI vol vs correlation with trend line), regime comparison bars, and correlation time series

name: Bond Reaction to Hot vs Cool CPI
description:
LOAD TLT daily bars and CPI from common_economic_indicators
DETECT CPI releases via valuewhen for prior readings
CLASSIFY as Hot (MoM acceleration: current change > prior change) vs Cool (deceleration)
COMPUTE day-of and post-20d returns by regime with cumulative event compounding
DASHBOARD shows event counts (summary table), regime comparison bars, window breakdown table, and event markers

name: TSLA Sentiment Shock vs Fundamental Deterioration
description:
LOAD TSLA daily bars, analyst ratings, quarterly earnings, and income_statement data
DETECT 3 event types: analyst downgrades, earnings misses (surprise < -2%), and margin contractions
CLASSIFY downgrades as pure sentiment (no recent fundamental event) vs concordant using barssince
COMPUTE day-of and post-5/10/20d returns by event type
DASHBOARD shows event counts (summary table), mean-reversion vs persistence bar chart, window breakdown table, and event markers

name: TSLA Short Interest Amplification of Post-Earnings Drift
description:
LOAD TSLA daily bars, quarterly earnings, and short_interest data
DETECT earnings events via eps_surprise_percent
CLASSIFY into 4 regimes: High SI Beat, Low SI Beat, High SI Miss, Low SI Miss (DTC > 3 threshold)
COMPUTE earnings day and post-5/10/20d returns by regime
DASHBOARD shows event counts (summary table), 4-way regime comparison bars, window breakdown table, and event markers with surprise + DTC details

name: TSLA Short Volume Spike Reversal by Price Context
description:
LOAD TSLA daily bars and short_volume data
DETECT short volume spikes: ratio > 1.15× its 20-day SMA
CLASSIFY by price context: Bullish (price > 50-SMA) vs Bearish
COMPUTE spike day and post-5/10/20d returns by context
DASHBOARD shows context counts (summary table), reversal comparison bars, window breakdown table, and event markers with short vol ratio

name: Treasury Bond Futures Intraday Reaction to CPI
description:
LOAD ZB futures 1Min bars via futures_continuation and CPI from common_economic_indicators
DETECT CPI release near 8:30 AM ET
CLASSIFY as Hot (MoM acceleration) vs Cool (deceleration)
COMPUTE 1-minute spike, 1-hour digestion, and full-day persistence returns by regime
DASHBOARD shows regime comparison bars, intraday window breakdown, cumulative return paths, and event markers

name: Corn/Soybean Ratio & Term Structure Dashboard
description:
LOAD ZC futures with NoAdjustment continuation and ZS via reference_futures piped through futures_continuation
COMPUTE C/S price ratio, 20d MA, seasonal norm (expanding mean per calendar month), seasonal deviation
EXTRACT term structure via cont.carry (front/back - 1) and cont.spread (M1 - M2 via spread_depth=1)
DASHBOARD shows snapshot summary table (current/high/low for ratio, seasonal, term structure), ratio line chart with MA and seasonal norm, carry and spread charts, and monthly seasonal bar chart
